authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
USDA Forest Service, Eldorado National Forest (Amador Ranger District)

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Cole Creek / Cole Creek Lakes not listed)

CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Cole Creek / Cole Creek Lakes not listed; sequence runs directly from "Cold Creek (Fresno Co.)" to "Convict Creek (Mono Co.)" with no intervening Cole Creek entry)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Mokelumne Wilderness — Eldorado National Forest, USDA Forest Service
sources.quotedText: Permits are mandatory for entry into the Mokelumne Wilderness year round for overnight use. California Department of Fish and Game licenses and regulations for fishing and hunting apply in the Mokelumne.

sources.title: Cole Creek Trail — Eldorado National Forest, USDA Forest Service
sources.quotedText: This trail travels from the Munson Meadow Trailhead through open forest and meadow to a series of lakes lying just above 8,000 foot elevation.

sources.title: Cole Creek Lakes Topo Map, Amador County, CA (Mokelumne Peak quad) — TopoZone
Cole Creek Lakes — GNIS Feature Detail (Amador County, CA)
sources.quotedText: Latitude: 38.5826156, Longitude: -120.091074, GNIS Feature ID: 258586, Feature Type: Lake, County: Amador, State: California.

parameters.note: Cole Creek Lakes is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Cole Creek Lakes lies within the Mokelumne Wilderness (Eldorado National Forest); a Wilderness Permit is mandatory year-round for overnight camping (day fishing trips do not require an overnight permit), and standard CDFW fishing licenses and regulations apply throughout the wilderness.

regulations.citation: USFS Eldorado National Forest — Mokelumne Wilderness permit requirements
regulations.humanSummary: Located in the Mokelumne Wilderness; a Wilderness Permit is required year-round for overnight camping, and CDFW fishing licenses/regulations apply
regulations.verbatimText: Permits are mandatory for entry into the Mokelumne Wilderness year round for overnight use. California Department of Fish and Game licenses and regulations for fishing and hunting apply in the Mokelumne.
